title: Feature: per-agent custom executable path in Settings (bypass PATH discovery)
prompt:
GOAL: Let the user specify an explicit executable PATH for each agent in Settings. When set, Kanban uses that absolute path for BOTH detection and launch, instead of relying on $PATH discovery. This fixes the field problem where Kanban run as a systemd daemon can't find CLI agents because the daemon's PATH doesn't include user-local install dirs (e.g. claude at /home/developer/.local/bin/claude). PATH injection into the unit was attempted and did not reliably solve it; a per-agent path override is the robust fix. KEEP the existing PATH-discovery logic as the default/fallback — this is additive.

WHY IT'S A SMALL, CLEAN CHANGE (verified facts):
- Each agent in the catalog has a bare 'binary' string: src/core/agent-catalog.ts (claude→'claude', codex→'codex', pi, opencode, droid, kiro-cli, gemini).
- Detection already supports ABSOLUTE PATHS: src/terminal/command-discovery.ts isBinaryAvailableOnPath() — if the string contains '/' or '\\' it does canAccessPath(thePath) directly (lines 51-53) instead of scanning $PATH. So feeding it a custom absolute path 'just works' for detection.
- Detection callers: src/terminal/agent-registry.ts — detectInstalledCommands() (maps catalog .binary), resolveAgentCommand() (builds the launch command from .binary + baseArgs), getCuratedDefinitions() (sets installed flag). The 'command'/'binary'/'args' it returns flow into launch.
- Launch: the resolved binary/command flows to src/terminal/session-manager.ts which spawns the agent.

REQUIRED IMPLEMENTATION:
1. Persistence: add an optional per-agent 'executablePath' (absolute path) override. Store it via the existing per-agent config path (src/agent-sdk/kanban/agent-provider-config.ts / committed-provider store / runtime-config), consistent with how other per-agent settings persist. Empty/unset = current behavior (discover 'binary' on $PATH).
2. Wire contract: add the field to the relevant agent settings/config schema in src/core/api-contract.ts so the frontend can read/write it.
3. Resolution: in src/terminal/agent-registry.ts, when an agent has an executablePath override, use THAT as the effective 'binary'/'command' for detectInstalledCommands(), resolveAgentCommand(), and the 'installed' flag — passing the absolute path through isBinaryAvailableOnPath (already supported). Fall back to the catalog bare binary + $PATH when no override.
4. Launch: ensure session-manager spawns the overridden absolute path. 
5. Settings UI: add a text input for 'Executable path' to each agent's settings surface (the per-agent Providers/agent settings section — follow AGENTS.md web-ui stack: Tailwind v4 / Radix / lucide / ui primitives). Show the detected/effective path; validate it points to an executable file (reuse server-side check); surface 'not found / not executable' inline.

IMPORTANT SECONDARY NOTE (call out in UI help text, don't necessarily solve here): a wrapper like ~/.local/bin/claude may have a '#!/usr/bin/env node' shebang, so even with the absolute claude path pinned, the SPAWNED process still needs 'node' on its PATH to actually run. Pinning the path fixes DETECTION and the claude binary location; if launch then fails for lack of node, that's the daemon-PATH issue (separate task 10c97). Consider whether the spawn env should also include the override binary's own dir on PATH.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Setting an absolute executable path for an agent makes it detected as installed and launchable even when its dir is NOT on the process $PATH (the daemon case).
- Leaving it blank preserves today's $PATH-discovery behavior exactly.
- Path round-trips/persists; invalid path surfaces an inline error.
- Unit tests: agent-registry resolution with override (absolute path) vs without; detection via override path.
- web:typecheck + biome/lint clean; no 'any'; SDK/existing types reused.
